skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Creates original p5.js generative art from a written algorithmic philosophy, using seeded randomness, interactive controls, and reusable HTML templates.
skill.summary: Create original, interactive p5.js generative art with seeded variation and parameter controls.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, credential access, and an obfuscation heuristic, but these were false positives from Markdown fences, art terminology, comments, and RegExp.exec usage. The only confirmed risk is external network loading of p5.js and Google Fonts in browser templates, which is a minor supply-chain and privacy dependency. No prompt injection, credential access, malicious intent, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: External CDN and Font Dependencies
low_findings.description: The generated HTML template loads p5.js and Google Fonts from public CDNs. This is legitimate for a browser-based art artifact, but it creates minor availability, privacy, and supply-chain exposure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are direct script and font dependencies used by the template. They do not send secrets or user data, so the risk is limited to external resource loading.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The command execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, Windows SAM, and dangerous-combination findings were not confirmed. The evidence points to Markdown code fences, creative-writing terms, comments, and JavaScript RegExp.exec for hex color parsing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no shell execution, Python exec, process execution, credential file access, or cryptographic operation at the flagged locations. The suspicious tokens occur in benign instructional text or normal JavaScript parsing code.

content.value_statement: Generative art requests often need repeatable code, controls, and a clear creative direction. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create seeded p5.js artworks.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic art philosophy before implementation.
Builds self-contained HTML artifacts using p5.js from a CDN.
Uses seeded randomness so the same seed can reproduce an artwork.
Adds sidebar controls for parameters such as counts, speed, scale, and colors.
Supports seed navigation, random seed selection, reset behavior, and browser viewing.
Provides template guidance for flow fields, particles, palettes, and p5.js structure.
limitations: Generated artifacts depend on browser support and external p5.js availability.
The included viewer template is a starting point, not a finished artwork.
It does not guarantee high frame rates for very large particle counts.
It should create original work and not imitate a living artist's exact style.

use_cases.title: Prototype Generative Art
use_cases.description: Turn a mood, theme, or abstract idea into an interactive p5.js sketch with repeatable seeds.

use_cases.title: Create Visual Explorations
use_cases.description: Build parameterized visual systems for presentations, concept work, or design research.

use_cases.title: Teach Computational Aesthetics
use_cases.description: Show how particles, flow fields, randomness, and controls shape generative art outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Artwork
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an original interactive algorithmic artwork about calm ocean currents. Include the philosophy, seeded p5.js HTML, and basic controls.

prompt_templates.title: Controlled Variation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a seeded p5.js generative artwork based on urban rain at night. Add seed navigation and explain how each seed changes the result.

prompt_templates.title: Parameter Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an algorithmic art artifact about magnetic fields. Include sliders for density, field strength, trail length, and color intensity.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Art Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a gallery-quality generative artwork about recursive growth and erosion. Use a clear philosophy, custom parameters, seeded variation, and a refined interface.

output_examples.input: Create a generative artwork about migrating birds and wind.
output_examples.output: A short algorithmic philosophy, followed by an interactive p5.js artwork with flocking motion, wind controls, color controls, and seed navigation.

output_examples.input: Make a calm abstract piece for a meditation page.
output_examples.output: A browser-ready HTML artifact with slow particles, soft palettes, reproducible seeds, reset controls, and adjustable motion intensity.

output_examples.input: Generate 100 variations of one visual system.
output_examples.output: A single seeded viewer that lets the user step through variations, jump to a seed, randomize the seed, and tune visual parameters.

best_practices: Start with a clear concept and let the algorithm express that concept.
Keep parameter ranges small enough to preserve performance and visual quality.
Use seeds to save, compare, and reproduce strong variations.
anti_patterns: Do not copy an existing artist's recognizable style or named work.
Do not replace the template with unrelated layouts unless the user asks.
Do not use random values without a reproducible seed.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates original algorithmic art concepts and interactive p5.js HTML artifacts.

faq.question: Does it create static images?
faq.answer: It focuses on interactive browser artifacts, but users can export images from the generated canvas.

faq.question: Why does it use seeds?
faq.answer: Seeds make random-looking artworks reproducible, so users can return to a specific result.

faq.question: Can I change the visual output?
faq.answer: Yes. The template supports sliders, color controls, seed navigation, and reset behavior.

faq.question: Does it require a web server?
faq.answer: No. The generated HTML is intended to open directly in a browser or artifact viewer.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with low risk. The confirmed concern is external CDN and font loading in browser templates.
